paper_title: Multicolor list Ramsey numbers grow exponentially
summary: Fox, He, Luo, and Xu (arXiv:2103.15175) established $\frac{1}{e}\cdot 2^k \leq R_\ell(K_3,k) \leq (4+o(1))^k$, proving exponential growth but leaving a gap between the constants $2/e$ and $4+o(1)$. The question of whether $\lim_{k\to\infty} R_\ell(H,k)^{1/k}$ even exists, let alone its value, remains open. A targeted web search (May 2026) found no follow-up paper that resolves the exponential constant for any fixed $H$.
search_queries: list Ramsey numbers exponential constant limit K3 multicolor Fox He Luo Xu 2103.15175
multicolor list Ramsey exponential growth rate triangle lim R_ell k 1/k
"list Ramsey" "exponential constant" OR "lim_{k" OR "growth rate" 2023 2024 2025 K_3 triangle
notes: No follow-up found. The closely related paper arXiv:2312.13965 (published in Research in the Mathematical Sciences 2024) determines the tower height of multicolor Ramsey numbers for 3-uniform hypergraphs in the classical (non-list) setting, but does not address list Ramsey numbers. The exact value of $\lim_{k\to\infty} R_\ell(K_3,k)^{1/k}$ — and even its existence — remains unknown.
